commit_msg: feat(now): introduce Now moments pipeline — capture the present in time
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Pipeline to Nowhere
subtitle: He built a machine to capture the present, but it only caught him.
when_where: 🌙 8:21 PM on main, the hour when developers start believing their own mythology. The terminal glowed like a confessional booth. 🔌 He was building a pipeline—not for data, but for *moments*. The kind of moments that slip away the moment you try to hold them.
who_whom: 🎯 The pipeline had one job: capture the present in time. But *now* is a moving target, and every attempt to freeze it only proves how much has already passed. 🧠 Omkar versus himself—the architect fighting his own creation, the moment he realized the machine would always be one frame behind, measuring what was already gone.
what_why: 💾 He introduced the Now moments pipeline with surgical precision—a data structure designed to hold the unholdable, to make the present *legible*. 🔍 But legibility came at a cost: every captured moment became evidence of every moment he *missed*. The pipeline didn't preserve the present; it proved the present was already a corpse by the time he touched it. 💀 He'd built the perfect machine for a man obsessed with proving he was still alive.
verdict: The pipeline doesn't capture now; it proves now is always already then.
